meta.title: BAXUS 2.0: Real-World Applications, Not Just Real-World Assets

transcript:
It is so great to be back at Breakpoint. Um, I remember the last time that we were here, uh, we actually went to Breakpoint number one, and we had no marketplace, we had no vault, uh, we had an idea. And I had actually just forked the Metaplex repo to get a subsidized dev ticket, and my co-founder Tzvi forged a COVID vaccine card to use his friend's pass. So we've come a long way, and I'm really happy to share with you all of the amazing progress we've made over the years. And the idea has stayed the, honestly, exact same since the beginning. The wine and spirits market is broken, Solana is the rails, and BAXUS is the solution.

At that point in time, there was no term for what we know today as Real-World Assets. Instead, what we were looking to do was just provide real value to our users. And we were trying to build a platform to solve these issues. And we knew that if Real-World Assets were ever going to exist on-chain, we had to build the infrastructure off-chain. And that isn't just software; that also means vaulting infrastructure, logistics, authentication processes, all these things that you have to do to deal with real assets.

So over the years, we have done a couple fun things. Uh, we've let you buy whiskey with your meme coins, with crypto. Uh, we've let you buy Lehman bottles via Blinks. And we've let you buy Drink Token. But please know that this is not financial advice, nor is the Drink Token the official token of BAXUS, nor is this any indication that we will ever launch an official token for BAXUS. Um, but that's just to the side. But today, I'm really, really proud to announce and present the next phase in what we're ushering in.

So, BAXUS as a company, you can think of us in three specific parts. We have our Vault, we have Athyna, which is our IoT DePIN layer, and then finally today, I'm going to be talking about our user layer and how we're bringing all of this together to unlock more consumer experiences and applications.

So to start off with our Vault, um, we have two gray, nondescript buildings in random places in New Jersey. Uh, and these are our physical custody and authentication facilities. Here we manage thousands of bottles, and we have verified logistics and storage processes. And we've been able to actually sell this as a service to other companies across the ecosystem. Like for example, if you were in the vending machine craze and you know about Emporium cards, we did all the vaulting for that company.

And so now, Athyna. So I actually, when I was still in college, presented in Breakpoint in 2022 about an idea for a trustless vaulted infrastructure, which just meant that we would take IoT devices, connect them to the Helium network, and then be able to connect that information about the location of the asset, how it's verified and authentic, and any environment variables to the actual on-chain representation. So as the end user, you are aware of everything that's going on affecting the real asset underneath. And today I'm really, really proud to announce that we have actually closed major partnerships across Kentucky, and we have been able to unlock the barrel market using this technology.

So we are now working with Diageo, which is a massive wine and spirits conglomerate, the Koetter Group in Kentucky, which is one of the largest rickhouse builders, and Bardstown Bourbon Company, which is one of the fastest growing distilleries in the world. And together, they actually have more than 10 billion dollars in barrel assets that we're going to be able to tokenize through this IoT hardware technology.

And then finally, we are moving from a marketplace into the exchange. And this is where all of our physical assets become programmable. So we're talking lending. We're talking an entirely new mobile experience that blends everything together. Um, we're talking vending machines. And everything is going to be able to be used by your grandmother. It's going to be very easy to access, easy to understand. And if you don't know about blockchain, you're still going to be able to buy whiskey. But if you are a crypto degen and you love these things, then you'll be able to have those experiences too.

And today, I'm very excited to announce that we are launching the first decentralized pricing for Real-World Assets only to the Seeker phone. So this now connects every single layer across the application. With Meb... sorry... oh my gosh... Web... Jesus... Mobile, IoT hardware, blockchain rails, and decentralized pricing, logistics... there's a lot of buzzwords here. But we're taking all this information and we're turning this into an actual consumer project. And so this is the way that we're able to take just the Real-World Asset of whiskey and turn it into a Real-World Application. Um, thank you so much. Uh, I'm really excited that you are all going to be able to do so much more than just buy whiskey.

enrichment.generated_query: BAXUS 2.0 BAXUS Solana Breakpoint 2025 announcement blog technical docs

sources.title: RISE Acquires BSX, a Perp DEX on Base, to Accelerate its Development of the First Integrated Orderbook Primitives
sources.snippet:
RISE Acquires BSX, a Perp DEX on Base, to Accelerate its Development of the First Integrated Orderbook Primitives
[![RISE Labs](https://blog.risechain.com/content/images/2025/02/Rise_Light-Logotype@2x.png)](https://blog.risechain.com)
[Sign in](#/portal/signin)[Subscribe](#/portal/signup)
[News](https://blog.risechain.com/tag/news/)Featured
# RISE Acquires BSX, a Perp DEX on Base, to Accelerate its Development of the First Integrated Orderbook Primitives
* [![Sasha Mai](https://blog.risechain.com/content/images/size/w100/2025/04/IMG_0302.JPG)](https://blog.risechain.com/author/sasha/)
#### [Sasha Mai](https://blog.risechain.com/author/sasha/)
11 Nov 2025•2 min read
![RISE Acquires BSX, a Perp DEX on Base, to Accelerate its Development of the First Integrated Orderbook Primitives](https://blog.risechain.com/content/images/size/w2000/2025/11/Acquisition-3.png)
*BSX Labs, backed by Blockchain Capital and Coinbase Ventures, brings expertise from supporting over $15B in orderbook trading volume. The team will help RISE develop EVM-based synchronously composable orderbook primitives that will bridge traditional financial markets.*
**Singapore –November 11, 2025**–[RISE](http://risechain.com/?ref=blog.risechain.com)today announced its acquisition of BSX Labs, the team behind the BSX perpetuals exchange on the Base network.
The BSX team’s experience in designing and operating a hybrid orderbook trading system positions RISE to accelerate innovation in fully onchain orderbook products, a core use case of RISE’s high-speed Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain. This collaboration will deliver Integrated Orderbook Primitives that benefit from synchronous composability with other DeFi applications, higher speed and throughput, and increased compatibility between blockchain and traditional financial markets; opening new opportunities for both retail and institutional users as well as app developers.
> "The BSX team built an impressive trading engine that attracted significant volume and a loyal user base," said **> Sam Battenally, CEO of RISE
**> . "Integrating their technology and talent is a key strategic step for us. It accelerates our roadmap towards launching a native, best-in-class orderbook infrastructure on RISE."
The market is yet to see fully composable orderbooks onchain and at scale. Existing solutions are either fragmented with custom execution, or the underlying chain cannot support such scale. With the immense forecasted growth of stablecoins and tokenization, RISE, together with the BSX team, is taking an opinionated bet that integrated perps and spot orderbooks will be the preferred infrastructure for brokers, asset issuers and retail traders.
With Integrated Orderbooks, retail brokers get access to deep liquidity, asset issuers can list spot and perps instruments for their assets; and users get access to the best execution, new assets and net-new DeFi yield opportunities.
BSX launched in 2023 and processed over $15B in cumulative trading volume. The acquisition will provide a path forward for BSX token holders, who will be eligible for an airdrop of RISE’s upcoming native token, with 1.5% of the RISE token total supply allocated to BSX tokens currently in circulation in the market.
> "Joining RISE is an incredible opportunity for our team and our community," said **> Avi, CEO of BSX Labs
**> . "We share a vision for a more performant and transparent onchain trading future. With the resources and ecosystem of RISE, we can now build that future at a much larger scale."
As part of the integration, the BSX decentralized exchange (DEX) will be sunsetted in a structured one-week process. The shutdown of the BSX DEX will commence on November 11, 2025 at 15:00 UTC. Users are advised to visit the[BSX blog](https://mirror.xyz/bsx.eth?ref=blog.risechain.com)for instructions on closing positions and withdrawing assets, and for further details.
**About BSX Labs:**
BSX Labs was the developer of the BSX Protocol, a decentralized perpetuals e

sources.title: Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained
sources.snippet:
Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained - NOUS RESEARCH[Skip to content](#content)
[](#elementor-action:action=popup:open&settings=eyJpZCI6IjE3NjEiLCJ0b2dnbGUiOmZhbHNlfQ==)
Menu
# Introducing Hermes 4.3: Local Intelligence Globally Trained
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/435_b_s_2-scaled.jpg)
Today we’re releasing Hermes 4.3 ([🤗 Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B)), an update to our flagship Hermes series of models. Hermes 4.3 was trained with an extended context length (up to 512K) and nearly matches (and in some cases exceeds) the performance of Hermes 4 70B at half the parameter cost. Based on[Seed-OSS-36B-Base](https://huggingface.co/ByteDance-Seed/Seed-OSS-36B-Base), Hermes 4.3 is an excellent shape for consumer local inference or enterprise self-deployment. The[GGUFs](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B-GGUF)comfortably sit in the VRAM of off-the-shelf GPUs, offering a private, powerful, neutrally-aligned model to everyone.
Hermes 4.3 is our first production model post-trained entirely on the[Psyche](https://psyche.network/)network, our distributed training network that uses the DisTrO optimizer to efficiently communicate between training nodes spread out through data centers over the open internet and secured by the consensus of the Solana blockchain. By enabling nodes throughout the world to collaborate on a single training run, Psyche can dramatically reduce the cost of training frontier level models, leveling the playing field for open source AI model developers.
Although we’ve performed extensive scientific research on DisTrO and its implementation in Psyche, we decided to verify its effectiveness for production workloads by training Hermes 4.3 both on Psyche and via the traditional centralized approach. As outlined in the[Hermes 4 Technical Report](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.18255), we used our[custom version of Torchitan](https://github.com/nousresearch/torchtitan/)to train Hermes 4.3 following the same recipe as Hermes 4 (FSDP+AdamW). We then trained the model a second time on Psyche (TP+DisTrO). Given the extended context length and increased training set size, the Hermes 4.3 training run was twice as large as Hermes 4.
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_12-scaled.png)
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_22-scaled.png)
The training run proved stable throughout, averaging 144k tokens/second spread across 24 Psyche nodes. Using DisTro’s overlapped collective strategy, the entirety of the P2P communications were hidden by the training time, effectively achieving equivalent throughput to traditional, centralized training. Under the hood, Psyche uses a dual L1-P2P networking model where consensus state is managed by a smart contract on the Solana blockchain while training gradients are communicated out-of-band through a custom mesh P2P network.
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_34_2-scaled.png)
**The Psyche trained version of Hermes 4.3 outperformed the traditional centralized version on a suite of downstream tasks**. Although previous runs had produced similar results, it was a confirming signal that Psyche is up to the task of training production models. Hermes 4.3 achieves SOTA on RefusalBench across all popular closed and open models in being helpful and aligned to the user’s values. For transparency we are publishing the full set of[evaluation responses and scorings](https://huggingface.co/datasets/NousResearch/eval-Hermes-4.3-36B). In addition we are releasing the centrally trained version ([🤗 Model](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-4.3-36B-centralized),[Evals](https://huggingface.co/datasets/NousResearch/eval-Hermes-4.3-36B-centralized)) as a research artifact (you’re welcome @xl8harder).
![](https://nousresearch.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/43_52-scaled.png)
To learn more about Psyche, check out the[live dashboard](https://psyche.network/)

tags.impact_reason: BAXUS 2.0's focus on real-world asset infrastructure and integration of IoT for supply chain tracking provides a solid update to the RWA space on Solana.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a premium whiskey bottle inside a futuristic vault as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript explicitly mentions 'Real-World Assets,' 'vaulting infrastructure,' and the 'wine and spirits market.' This anchor directly represents the physical, high-value assets BAXUS secures. For the homepage text, I integrated '$10B' from the headline, rendering it as a holographic data point within the vault's security system. This diegetically communicates the market scale BAXUS is unlocking, blending the financial narrative with the physical reality of the asset.
visual_idea.description: A low-angle shot of a single, exquisitely shaped bottle of amber whiskey, displayed like a museum piece inside a secure, brushed titanium vault. Attached to the bottle's neck is a subtle, glowing IoT device representing 'Athyna.' From the pedestal below, a crisp, blue holographic interface projects vital data, with the characters '$10B' clearly visible and integrated into the security display. The background recedes into the atmospheric depth of a vast, modern vault, with rows of identical, secured assets softly out of focus.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, low-angle shot, composed vertically (3:4 aspect ratio), emphasizing extreme height and epic scale with substantial negative space at the top. A single, exquisitely shaped bottle of amber whiskey, displayed like a museum piece, resides inside a secure, grand brushed titanium vault. The brushed titanium surfaces exhibit a subtle metallic sheen with distinct directional reflections and deep shadows that highlight its textured finish. Attached to the bottle's neck is a subtle, glowing IoT device representing 'Athyna', emitting a soft, internal luminescence. From the pedestal below, a crisp, blue holographic interface projects vital data, with the characters '$10B' clearly visible and seamlessly integrated into the security display, glowing with a vibrant, ethereal light and casting faint blue ambient light onto the surrounding surfaces. The background recedes into the atmospheric depth of a vast, modern vault, with rows of identical, secured assets softly out of focus. The scene is lit with dramatic, ultra-photorealistic lighting, featuring sharp reflections and prominent specular highlights on the polished glass bottle and the holographic projection. Cool, diffused ambient light subtly illuminates the vault, contrasting with precise, focused light on the whiskey bottle, enhancing its form and revealing intricate refractions and glossy reflections of the polished glass. The overall atmosphere is high-end, cinematic, and technically pristine, with subtle depth-of-field. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, high dynamic range, cinematic lighting, photorealistic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a premium whiskey bottle inside a futuristic vault as the Semantic Anchor because the transcript explicitly mentions 'Real-World Assets,' 'vaulting infrastructure,' and the 'wine and spirits market.' This anchor directly represents the physical, high-value assets BAXUS secures. For the homepage text, I integrated '$10B' from the headline, rendering it as a holographic data point within the vault's security system. This diegetically communicates the market scale BAXUS is unlocking, blending the financial narrative with the physical reality of the asset.
visual_idea.description: A macro, top-down shot focusing on the intricate crystal stopper of the premium whiskey bottle. Embedded just beneath the surface of the stopper is the 'Athyna' IoT device, its micro-circuitry glowing with a soft, pulsing light. The image captures the fusion of luxury craftsmanship and advanced DePIN technology, with the cool, metallic texture of the vault interior creating a soft-focus bokeh effect in the background.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, top-down, hyper-detailed shot, perfectly centered and symmetrical, filling the 1:1 square frame. The focus is on the intricate, multifaceted crystal stopper of a premium whiskey bottle. The crystal exhibits the brilliant, sharp reflections and refractions of polished glass, with subtle internal light play and specular highlights, as if catching soft, cool daylight. Embedded just beneath its surface is the 'Athyna' IoT device, its micro-circuitry glowing with a soft, pulsing blue light, resembling a holographic interface. The name 'Athyna' is subtly etched and illuminates from within the device's surface, reflecting the qualities of a digital sentry. This fusion of luxury craftsmanship and advanced DePIN technology is rendered with exceptional clarity. In the background, a cool, brushed titanium texture of a vault interior is present, creating a soft-focus bokeh effect, enhancing the depth and emphasizing the central subject. The lighting is soft and cool, diffused to highlight every minute detail and texture, while also accentuating the device's glow and the crystal's sharp reflections. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range.

editorial.headline: BAXUS Unlocks $10B Spirits Market with Diageo Partnership and Seeker Integration
editorial.standfirst: The platform's new IoT layer 'Athyna' and exclusive Solana Mobile integration mark a pivot from simple tokenization to a programmable exchange for global spirits giants.
key_takeaways: Partnerships with Diageo, Koetter Group, and Bardstown Bourbon Company unlock over $10B in barrel assets.
Launch of 'Athyna,' a DePIN IoT layer utilizing the Helium network for trustless physical custody.
Exclusive decentralized pricing and lending features launching on the Solana Seeker phone.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The narrative around Real-World Assets (RWAs) often stalls at the tokenization phase—creating a digital receipt for a physical good. At Breakpoint 2025, **BAXUS** signaled a maturity leap for the sector, moving beyond simple marketplaces to full-stack institutional infrastructure. Co-founder Carrie Kellar revealed a suite of major partnerships with **Diageo**, **The Koetter Group**, and **Bardstown Bourbon Company**, a coalition that brings over **$10 billion** in barrel assets onto the BAXUS rails. This is no longer a retail experiment; it is the digitization of the global spirits supply chain.

Central to this expansion is the launch of **Athyna**, an IoT DePIN layer that solves the critical "oracle problem" for physical goods. By leveraging the Helium network, Athyna connects the physical state of assets—location, temperature, and authenticity—directly to their on-chain representation. For institutional partners like Koetter (a major rickhouse builder) and Bardstown (one of the world's fastest-growing distilleries), this offers a trustless, automated audit trail that traditional logistics cannot match. The infrastructure transforms static "assets" into dynamic "applications," capable of reacting to real-world environmental variables in real-time.

The pivot from a marketplace to a full exchange model introduces programmable liquidity to these commodities. BAXUS is rolling out lending markets and a mobile-first experience that bridges the gap between institutional custody and DeFi utility. In a strategic move to capture the mobile crypto user, Kellar announced that the first decentralized pricing for these RWAs will be available exclusively on the **Solana Seeker** phone. This integration creates a unique hardware-software loop, where the Seeker serves as the terminal for high-fidelity, real-time valuation of liquid assets.

While the platform continues to cater to crypto-natives with features like "Lehman Brothers" bottle drops and meme coin payments, the underlying message is clear: the rails are now robust enough for the Fortune 500. By securing the supply chain of industry giants like Diageo, BAXUS has effectively graduated from a niche Web3 vertical to a legitimate financial layer for the global spirits economy.
